steps.title: Write the odd integers
steps.content: Consecutive odd integers differ by $2$. If the first integer is $x$, then the four integers are $$x,\quad x+2,\quad x+4,\quad x+6$$

steps.title: Translate the comparison
steps.content: The fourth integer is $x+6$, so the product of $9$ and the fourth integer is $9(x+6)$. The sum of the first and third, with $18$ subtracted, is $x+(x+4)-18$.

steps.title: Use at most
steps.content: The phrase at most means less than or equal to, so the inequality is $$9(x+6)\le x+(x+4)-18$$ The answer is A.
